samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a RAM array with a depth of 8 and a width of 6 bits. Initialize all data to 0.

reg [5:0] RAM [0:8-1];

Define state and data registers.

reg [1:0] state, next_state; reg [2:0] read_addr_reg, write_addr_reg; reg [5:0] read_data_reg, write_data_reg;

Define a combinational logic block to handle write operations.

always @(*) begin if (rst_n) begin if (write_en) begin RAM[write_addr] = write_data; end end end

Define a combinational logic block to handle read operations.

always @(*) begin if (rst_n) begin if (read_en) begin read_data_reg = RAM[read_addr]; end else begin read_data_reg = 6'b0; end end end

Define a sequential logic block to handle clock operations.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (!rst_n) begin state <= STATE_IDLE; read_addr_reg <= 3'b0; write_addr_reg <= 3'b0; read_data_reg <= 6'b0; write_data_reg <= 6'b0; next_state <= STATE_IDLE; end else begin state <= next_state; end end
